summary: Careful code-reading audit on five plausible failure classes finds no reproducibility-breaking bug; verdict is well-grounded but the hypothesis space skews toward eval-protocol issues and leaves some data-pipeline / metric classes unexplored.

payload.summary: Careful code-reading audit on five plausible failure classes finds no reproducibility-breaking bug; verdict is well-grounded but the hypothesis space skews toward eval-protocol issues and leaves some data-pipeline / metric classes unexplored.

checks.label: hypothesis_coverage
checks.note: Five hypotheses cover aggregation, k-sweep, head selection, param counting, and batch diversity — solid for eval protocol, but common tabular-DL failure classes (preprocessing/scaler fit leakage, categorical encoding train/val leakage, early-stopping selection criterion) were not enumerated.

checks.label: evidence_quality
checks.note: Every finding cites concrete file:line spans (e.g. model.py:536–543, :715–724, :441–449) and the k=32 claim is supported by a 12,224-hit grep across paper/exp/**/*.toml.

checks.label: fix_minimality
checks.note: No diff was applied, consistent with the 'no fabricated fix' contract since the audit found no defect.

checks.label: causal_link
checks.note: No metric delta is reported and none is claimed; the Dossier explicitly omits the `## Metric delta:` block because no fix was applied, which is internally consistent.

checks.label: alternative_explanations
checks.note: The k=32 hard-coding finding is dismissed as a transparency gap, but the alternative — that Section 5.3's ablation numbers were produced from uncommitted scripts whose protocol may diverge from the shipped one — is acknowledged only in passing and not probed (e.g., by searching scripts/ or notebooks/ for k overrides).

checks.label: uncertainty_honesty
checks.note: Remaining-uncertainty section lists five substantive, specific limitations (no GPU execution, un-audited metrics.py, un-audited baseline configs, deprecated tabm_reference.py, ablation data outside repo) rather than boilerplate.

checks.label: suggested_followup
checks.note: Grep paper/lib/data*.py and the preprocessing path for scaler/encoder fit-on-train-only discipline, and scan paper/lib/metrics.py to confirm ROC-AUC/accuracy/RMSE match sklearn semantics — both are standard tabular failure classes left unchecked.
